title: Overview Customizable Enterprise Workspace Builder

entry_points: Customize Layout button in the Overview command center
floating Customize Layout button when the workspace is locked for normal use
edit_mode_behavior: Overview enters a locked/unlocked layout editor mode
normal dashboard controls remain clean when edit mode is off
edit mode shows a compact toolbar with Done, Save Layout, Reset Layout, Add Panel, and Lock Layout
tiles show drag handles, resize handles, and hide controls only in edit mode

tiles: Snapshot Library Shortcut / Saved Snapshot Status

grid.overlap_resolution: layout changes are clamped and overlapping tiles are pushed to the next available grid rows

draggable_resizable_tiles.browser_smoke_note: The in-app browser verified rendered edit handles and toolbar controls. Its automation surface did not provide a reliable pointer drag stream for mechanical drag/resize proof, so manual drag/resize QA is still recommended even though the pointer-event code path is implemented.

save_reset_behavior.save_layout: writes the normalized custom layout to localStorage
save_reset_behavior.reset_layout: restores the default enterprise layout, persists it, clears the selected tile, scrolls the workspace viewport back to top, and dispatches a resize event for the map
save_reset_behavior.lock_layout: persists the locked layout and removes edit handles

tile_constraints.map: required tile; cannot be permanently hidden; has large minimum width and height for SceneView usability
tile_constraints.command_center: compact tile with limited height so it cannot become a giant dashboard block by accident
tile_constraints.intelligence: readable minimum width with standard and wide layout support through grid resizing
tile_constraints.layers: can be hidden, restored, narrowed, or widened while retaining all existing layer controls
tile_constraints.model_lab_controls: hidden by default, available through Add Panel and automatically surfaced in Model Lab mode
tile_constraints.snapshot_helper: hidden by default, available through Add Panel and automatically surfaced in Snapshot Builder mode

add_panel_and_hide_behavior.map_protection: Add Panel explains that the map is required and stays available; hide is blocked for the map tile
add_panel_and_hide_behavior.restore_controls: hidden panels can be restored through Add Panel

mode_aware_behavior.parcel: surfaces Active Selection and keeps Selected Parcel Intelligence in the right tile
mode_aware_behavior.countywide: surfaces the Layers tile and Countywide Intelligence
mode_aware_behavior.modelLab: surfaces Model Lab Controls and Model Lab Intelligence while preserving the overlay-off default
mode_aware_behavior.snapshot: surfaces Snapshot Helper and Snapshot Capture actions
mode_aware_behavior.principle: layout customization changes arrangement only; it does not change the underlying parcel, layer, model, or snapshot data logic

sceneview_preservation_notes: SceneView remains inside a stable Map / SceneView tile instead of being recreated by layout toolbar interactions
layout drag, resize, reset, save, and lock actions dispatch resize events so the map can refresh to the new tile size
the reset behavior now scrolls the workspace viewport back to top so the restored default layout is immediately visible
existing selected-parcel focus and map layer logic were left intact

workflow_preservation.search_parcel: browser smoke verified Search Parcel focuses the parcel search input and keeps parcel intelligence available
workflow_preservation.countywide: browser smoke verified Explore Countywide shows the Layers tile and Countywide Intelligence
workflow_preservation.model_lab: browser smoke verified Model Lab mode surfaces the model controls tile and Model Lab Intelligence without unsafe score/probability labels
workflow_preservation.snapshot_builder: browser smoke verified Snapshot Builder surfaces the Snapshot Helper tile and save/open snapshot actions without immediate snapshot creation
workflow_preservation.planning_snapshot: browser smoke verified the Planning Snapshot Library loads
workflow_preservation.methodology: browser smoke verified Methodology still loads; Methodology layout was not redesigned for this phase

notes: Phase 25A introduced layout state only and did not add model outputs or prediction endpoints.

Safety keyword scan over src and backend/app found existing internal research schema/repository field names and safety flags from prior Model Lab work; no new layout builder code exposes probabilities or raw scores.
Visible browser smoke found no prediction_probability or raw_model_score text in Model Lab mode.

validation_results.python_m_compileall_app: exit 0, but repository has no top-level app package to list

smoke_test_results.edit_mode_toolbar: passed; Done, Save Layout, Reset Layout, Add Panel, and Lock Layout rendered
smoke_test_results.edit_handles: passed; Map / SceneView drag and resize handles rendered
smoke_test_results.add_panel_menu: passed; hidden/restorable tiles listed and map protection message shown
smoke_test_results.save_layout: passed; toolbar showed Layout Saved after clicking Save Layout
smoke_test_results.reset_layout: passed; default map, command, and intelligence tiles restored; scroll-to-top behavior patched
smoke_test_results.search_parcel: passed; search input focused with aria-label Search parcels
smoke_test_results.countywide_mode: passed; Map Layers and Countywide Intelligence visible
smoke_test_results.model_lab_mode: passed; Model Lab Intelligence visible and model controls tile present
smoke_test_results.snapshot_builder_mode: passed; Snapshot Helper tile present with Save Snapshot/Open Snapshot actions
smoke_test_results.browser_console: not captured; the in-app browser wrapper did not expose console message retrieval in this session
smoke_test_results.drag_resize_interaction: partially verified; handles rendered and implementation code is present, but in-app browser automation could not deliver a reliable pointer drag stream for end-to-end drag movement

remaining_known_limitations: Manual drag/resize QA should be performed in a normal browser because the in-app browser automation surface did not support a reliable pointer drag stream during smoke testing.
The previous quick layout preset controls remain available as secondary controls, but the new Customize Layout editor is now the primary enterprise customization path.
Snapshots intentionally do not capture UI tile positions; they remain planning-context artifacts, not layout artifacts.
